prompt: What was Nixon referring to when he said, "I am not a crook"?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: When Richard Nixon said, "I am not a crook," he was referring to the Watergate scandal. This statement was made during a press conference on November 17, 1973, in response to questions about his involvement in the scandal and allegations of financial improprieties. Nixon was asserting his innocence regarding any illegal activities related to the Watergate break-in and subsequent cover-up.
